title: 7 Logging in to PaymentStream AFT
body_text:
7	Logging in to PaymentStream AFT
7.1	Overview of Logging in to PaymentStream AFT
PaymentStreamâ¢
AFT is a product on the
PaymentStream
Direct platform. Your financial institutionâs users access
PaymentStream
Direct via the Client Centre and they donât require a soft or hard token to do so.
Business member users access
PaymentStream
AFT via a direct URL provided by your financial institution. They also require a soft or a hard token. Quick-reference sheets for soft and hard token access are available for distribution. Your financial institution enters the unique AFT URL on the quick-reference sheets before distributing these to business members.
Related Information
Hard Token Access to PaymentStream AFT,
Form 6429
Soft Token Access to PaymentStream AFT,
Form 6430
7.2	Logging in to PaymentStream AFT â Financial Institution Users
Your financial institutionâs users proceed as follows to log in to
PaymentStream
AFT.
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 1.
Note: 	Youâll be prompted to enter your username before this page appears if itâs your first login to the Client Centre, youâve deleted your cookies, or youâre using incognito/private browsing.
Figure 1: Client Centre Login
Select
Applications
, then select
Production Applications
. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Select
PaymentStream Direct
. See Figure 3.
Note: 	Applications are grouped by type (e.g., Admin & Support, Payments). If needed, scroll down the page to find the application. If youâve saved the application as a favourite, it will also appear under âMy Favouritesâ.
Figure 3: Accessing PaymentStream Direct
Select
Perform Automated Fund Transfer
. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Access to PaymentStream AFT
END
